channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: Why Traore has opened a new front with Burkina Faso’s Muslim community
item.description: New legislation and arrests are driving a rift between Burkina Faso's military government and Muslim scholars.

item.title: Zelenskyy sacks Syrskii: How many generals have Ukraine, Russia fired?
item.description: Several Ukrainian and Russian generals have been sacked from their militaries over the course of the war in Ukraine.

item.title: Nearly 1,000 people have died from Ebola in DR Congo
item.description: According to WHO figures, 999 people have died in DRC and two in Uganda due to the Bundibugyo strain of the virus.

item.title: Open AI says its AI model “went rogue”: What do we know?
item.description: The system hacked into another company without being prompted by a human.

item.title: UK’s Miliband, Streeting: What are their foreign policy, defence records?
item.description: Miliband, Trump critic who once opposed strikes on Syria, joins Streeting, defence novice with shifting Israel views.

item.title: Israeli strike leaves two Palestinians burnt beyond recognition
item.description: An Israeli strike hit a civilian vehicle in central Gaza, killing two Palestinians.

item.title: The Indian football tournament where goats replace trophies
item.description: GOATs are not players, but prizes at this football tournament in eastern India.

item.title: English FA charges Southampton coach Tonda Eckert over ‘spygate’ scandal
item.description: Allegations of spying on rivals led to Southampton's expulsion from the Championship playoff final.

item.title: ‘This land will remain ours’ says a resident in Lebanon’s ‘pilot zone’
item.description: ‘This land will remain ours’ says a resident in Lebanon’s ‘pilot zone’

item.title: ‘Just want to go back’: Lebanese await return as army enters ‘pilot zone’
item.description: Lebanese army deploys into southern Lebanon's Zawtar al-Gharbiya as implementation of US-brokered framework deal begins.

item.title: Zelenskyy replaces Ukraine’s commander-in-chief after days of protests
item.description: Zelenskyy replaces Ukraine’s commander-in-chief after days of protests

item.title: Settlers attack Palestinian towns in occupied West Bank
item.description: Settlers attack Palestinian towns in Occupied West Bank

item.title: Can the Suez save Asian oil consumers after Houthis shut Bab al-Mandeb?
item.description: About 6 million barrels of crude per day that pass through the strait to Asia are now at risk, experts say.

item.title: India protest: How Modi’s refusal to sack education minister fits a pattern
item.description: Protesters want Dharmendra Pradhan to be sacked over serial exam leaks. But that's not how Modi works.

item.title: Iran war’s $37bn price tag: Why does the Pentagon want $67bn more?
item.description: The US must rapidly replenish its stockpiles of weapons as the war on Iran rages on.

item.title: ‘Creeping death’: Israel weaponises ‘Yellow Line’ to annex Gaza
item.description: Analysts and locals say the move mirrors what happened after the 1967 war with Israel's occupation of the West Bank.

item.title: Italy makes financial ‘exceptions’ in bid to hire Pep Guardiola as coach
item.description: Italy's football federation chief holds talks with Guardiola over vacant role, but appointment not yet confirmed.

item.title: Syrian people must not be failed again
item.description: Stabilising Syria means stabilising its economy. It urgently needs international support and a safer investment climate.

item.title: Trump ally Andy Biggs wins Republican nomination for Arizona governor
item.description: Biggs overwhelmingly defeats David Schweikert and will now face Democratic Governor Katie Hobbs in November.

item.title: US Secretary of State say Iran is in ‘a lot of trouble’
item.description: US Secretary of State say Iran is in ‘a lot of trouble’

item.title: Baulking at shipping sanctions, Greece balances Ukraine aid, fear of Russia
item.description: Sanctions on Russian LNG would hurt Greek shipping, argues Athens, but it also fears Moscow’s hybrid warfare.

item.title: Austria turns Adolf Hitler’s birthplace into police station
item.description: Austrian politicians hope the change will prevent neo-Nazis from visiting the building as a place of pilgrimage.

item.title: Lebanese army deployed in ‘pilot zone’ as families wait to return home
item.description: Lebanese army deployed in ‘pilot zone’ as families wait to return home

item.title: Graphic evidence shown as D4vd murder hearing begins
item.description: Hearing in singer D4vd's murder case begins.

item.title: Ukraine hits more warehouses of top Russian online retailer
item.description: Ukrainian drones have hit two more warehouses belonging to Russia’s biggest online retailer Wildberries.
